servinfosub.title: Arm
servinfosub.ptxt: Check body of suspension arm (1) for damage and deterioration. Replace suspension arm if check result is not OK.

servinfosub.title: Bushing
servinfosub.ptxt: Check suspension arm front bushing (2) for damage, wear and deterioration. Replace bushing if check result is not OK.
Check suspension arm rear busing (3) for damage, wear and deterioration. Replace suspension arm assembly if check result is not OK.

s1.ptxt: Remove front wheel.
Using washers (2) and other nuts (3) than the wheel nut, fix front brake disc (1) at diagonally opposing two studs.

attention2.ptxt: If the wheel nut is used to fix the front brake disc, the tapered end of the wheel nut may damage.
Do not use wheel nut for the brake disc fixation in this step.

s1.ptxt: Set dial gauge (2) using magnetic stand (1) placed on front brake disc.

attention3.ptxt: Apply measuring tip of dial gauge perpendicular to bottom center surface of ball joint as much as possible.

s1.ptxt: Set pointer to “0” (zero) by turning dial (3).
Push suspension arm up at near ball joint by hand with force of about 20 to 30 N (2.0 to 3.1 kgf) and hold this state for 2 to 3 seconds, and then read dial gauge.
Reversing direction of force, pull suspension arm down at near ball joint by hand with force of about 20 to 30 N (2.0 to 3.1 kgf) and hold this state for 2 to 3 seconds, and then read dial gauge.
Check total “a” of readings in steps 5) and 6), amount of looseness.
If measured looseness exceeds specified range below, replace suspension arm assembly.

spec.title: Amount of looseness in suspension arm ball joint

s1.ptxt: Lightly push ball joint dust cover (1) with fingers to check whether there is water inside.
If water is found to come out, replace suspension arm assembly.
Push top end (1) of ball joint dust cover down to check for rust on ball joint stud’s surface (2) in contact with dust cover.
If surface is found rusted, replace suspension arm assembly.
Move top end of ball joint dust cover back to original position.
Check ball joint dust cover (1) for cracks and damage.
If it is cracked or otherwise damaged, replace suspension arm assembly.
Detach ball joint stud from steering knuckle.
Check that ball joint stud (1) moves without sticking. If not, replace suspension arm assembly.
Connect suspension arm ball joint stud to steering knuckle.
Remove nuts and washers holding the front brake disc.
Install front wheel and tighten wheel nuts to specified torque.
